header.needsFloorTooltip: Add at least one Floor service to continue

priority.label: Priority
priority.popupP1: The auto-staffing solver fills days in priority order: Priority 1 first, then Priority 2, and so on.
priority.popupP2: Use it to guarantee coverage of the most important days (Friday night, Sunday brunch…) when staff is short — the solver favours these days when it has to make trade-offs.
priority.popupP3: Several days can share the same priority — they are then handled together.

copyDay.instruction: Click the target day to paste {{day}}

missing.explainKitchen: {{day}} has at least one floor service but no kitchen service. The restaurant cannot run without a kitchen team that day.
missing.explainFloor: {{day}} has at least one kitchen service but no floor service. The restaurant cannot run without a floor team that day.
missing.actionKitchen: Drag a Classic or Split card onto the {{day}} column in the Kitchen view to fill in.
missing.actionFloor: Drag a Classic or Split card onto the {{day}} column in the Floor view to fill in.

warning.title: HCR non-compliance
warning.amplitudeExplain: The total span between start and end of the day (splits included) must not exceed 13 hours. Reduce the span or shift the hours.
warning.coupureExplain: The split between two work periods must not exceed 5 hours. Shorten the split.

footer.help: Drag to create · Edges ↕ time · Grip → headcount · Double-click to edit

namePrompt.title: Name the profile
namePrompt.description: Give a name to this profile to save it. Otherwise it will be applied only to the selected week.

toast.profileNotFound: Profile not found - it may have been changed
toast.nameRequired: Give a name to this target

edit.name: Name
edit.nameTaken: This name already exists for this role

intro.title: Why define a core team?
intro.p1: Until Comptoir has historical data on your restaurant, auto-staffing has no idea who you usually placed in each slot. The generated schedule remains viable but may diverge significantly from the habits established on paper — a source of friction for your teams when switching over.
intro.p2: By designating a core team, you give the solver a reference schedule that it favours. It is a guideline, not a strict rule: if a stronger constraint requires it (leave, unavailability, hours cap, HCR legal rest…), it will substitute another employee to produce a valid week.
intro.p3: After about 12 weeks of real scheduling, the model has enough history to infer these habits automatically and follow your team's natural evolution. Core team assignments then become optional and can be lightened.
intro.drag: Drag employees onto the slots to define the core team for each day.

errors.noTargets: No staffing target defined for this profile. First define services and headcount on the Target page.
